mnemonics.title: GBN vs SR Remember
mnemonics.content: GBN: Gone Back iN - Go Back to N when error. SR: Selective Repeat - Select and Repeat only the bad one.

mnemonics.title: TCP Window States
mnemonics.content: ZAPS: Zero window Advertised, Probe sent, Sender waits.

mnemonics.title: Flow Control Purpose
mnemonics.content: FLOW: Fast sender Leaves Overflows Withholding data - regulates rate to prevent overwhelming receiver.

acronyms.usage: Time for packet to travel to receiver and acknowledgment to return to sender, crucial for flow control timing.
TCP field indicating available buffer space at receiver for flow control.
Message sent by receiver to confirm receipt of frames/data.
Message indicating specific frame was not received correctly, used in some flow control protocols.
Protocol that automatically retransmits data when errors are detected, includes flow control.
Maximum number of unacknowledged frames the sender can transmit.
Maximum number of frames receiver can accept/buffer.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a train of sequence numbers as stations along a track. The window is like a moving observation car that can see only a limited number of stations (frames) at a time. As acknowledgment arrives, the window slides forward, revealing new stations.
Stop-and-Wait is like one person firing a gun and waiting to see if it hit before loading the next bullet. Continuous is like a machine gun firing rounds continuously.
Think of a bus with limited seats (buffer). When full (zero window), no new passengers (data) can board until some passengers leave (data consumed).
Like a teacher marking wrong answer on page 3 and asking student to redo pages 3, 4, 5, 6...
Like a teacher marking only wrong answer on page 3 and asking student to redo only page 3.

analogies.analogy: Flow control is like a toll booth on a highway. The toll booth (receiver) can only process a certain number of cars per minute. If cars arrive faster than they can be processed, a backup queue forms. The toll booth signals cars to slow down or stop to prevent overflow.
Sliding window is like a sliding door with a fixed opening. You can see and process multiple people (frames) through the open window at once, but the window position limits how many you can handle.

TCP window advertisement is like a restaurant telling customers how many empty seats they have. Customers (senders) can only send that many orders (data packets) before waiting for more seats to open up.
Buffer overflow in flow control is like a mailbox that gets flooded with letters faster than the postal worker can collect them. Without flow control, letters would be lost.
